name: AGU A Ramen Bistro
tagline: Solid Honolulu ramen chain with rich tonkotsu broth — reliable bowl every single time.
mustOrder: Kotteri ramen — thick, rich tonkotsu broth with chashu and soft egg
AGU shoyu ramen — soy-based broth, lighter but still flavorful
Gyoza — pan-fried crispy, good starter while you wait
parking: Strip mall parking lot. The McCully location usually has spots available.
talkStory: AGU has expanded to multiple locations across Honolulu but the quality stays consistent, which is impressive. The tonkotsu broth is legit rich and porky. Not the absolute best ramen on the island (that's a heated debate), but it's reliably good and you rarely have to wait as long as some other spots. Multiple locations means there's usually one near you. Good rainy day option when you want something warm and filling.
